edge_hypothesis: The entire supertrend knowledge base is built on 4h data only. On 4h: champion PF=1.921 (8:1 R:R, ADX>10, 85 trades), ranging PF=2.91. But 1h has 4x more bars, producing potentially 300-400 trades vs 85-99 on 4h. The supertrend direction flip signal (SUPERTd_7_3.0) is a volatility-band crossover that should work on any timeframe — the question is whether the ranging profit engine translates to shorter timeframes. On 1h, ranging periods contain more bars with micro-reversions that supertrend can capture. MACD on 1h showed 147-170 trades vs 139 on 4h (only ~20% more, not 4x, because signal frequency doesn't scale linearly with bar count). For supertrend, the direction flip is sparse by nature, so 1h may produce 150-200 trades (2x, not 4x). If the 1h ranging PF stays above 2.0 and overall PF exceeds 1.5, supertrend_1h becomes the most statistically robust strategy in the portfolio AND a genuine timeframe diversifier against the 4h champion. Also testing whether the 8:1 R:R peak holds on 1h or shifts — shorter bars may favor tighter R:R (6:1) because 1h price moves are proportionally smaller relative to ATR. Invalidation: if 1h PF < 1.2 across all R:R ratios, the supertrend signal degrades on shorter timeframes (too much noise in direction flips).

variants.name: supertrend_1h_discovery_8to1
variants.description: Supertrend on ETH/1h with proven-optimal ADX>10 and 8:1 R:R. The direct 1h replication of the 4h champion. Stop 1.5x ATR, TP 12.0x ATR. If PF approaches 1.9 with 150+ trades, this is the new system champion by statistical robustness. If PF drops significantly from 4h, the supertrend signal degrades on 1h and the 4h champion is timeframe-specific. Expected: 150-200 trades, PF 1.3-1.9 depending on how well the ranging engine translates.
entry_long: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from <= 0 to > 0 (supertrend direction change to bullish).
ADX_14 > 10.0 (proven optimal ADX gate — captures ranging signals where profit engine lives).
entry_short: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from >= 0 to < 0 (supertrend direction change to bearish).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
ADX gate: ADX_14 > 10.0 (proven optimal from 4h testing)
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Direct 1h replication of 4h champion (PF=1.921). Does the peak R:R hold on shorter timeframe?
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 12.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: SUPERTd_7_3.0 direction flip — primary entry signal on 1h (untested timeframe)
ADX_14 > 10 — proven optimal from 4h, testing if same threshold works on 1h
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 12.0x TP — 8:1 R:R replicating 4h champion on 1h

variants.name: supertrend_1h_discovery_5to1
variants.description: Supertrend on ETH/1h with ADX>10 and moderate 5:1 R:R. Stop 1.5x ATR, TP 7.5x ATR. On 4h, 5:1 achieved PF=1.395 (ACCEPT). On 1h, shorter holding periods may favor tighter R:R because 1h ATR captures smaller moves — a 7.5x ATR TP on 1h may be hit more frequently than on 4h (where ATR is proportionally larger). If 1h at 5:1 exceeds the 4h PF=1.395, the 1h R:R curve peaks lower (favoring tighter targets). This would mean 1h supertrend is a different strategy archetype — high-frequency moderate-gain — rather than just a faster version of the 4h tail harvester.
entry_long: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from <= 0 to > 0 (supertrend direction change to bullish).
entry_short: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from >= 0 to < 0 (supertrend direction change to bearish).

Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Moderate R:R testing whether 1h favors tighter targets than 4h.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 7.5x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: SUPERTd_7_3.0 direction flip — testing signal quality on 1h
ADX_14 > 10 — optimal gate from 4h applied to 1h
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 7.5x TP — 5:1 R:R, testing if 1h curve peaks lower than 4h

variants.name: supertrend_1h_moderate_adx15_6to1
variants.description: Supertrend on ETH/1h with moderate ADX>15 and 6:1 R:R. Stop 1.5x ATR, TP 9.0x ATR. This variant adds a second dimension: does the 1h timeframe need a DIFFERENT ADX threshold than 4h? On 4h, ADX>10 was optimal but ADX>5 degraded to PF=1.220. On 1h, the higher noise level may require slightly more directional strength (ADX>15) to filter false supertrend flips. Combined with 6:1 R:R (below both the 4h peak at 8:1 and the moderate at 5:1) to test the complete lower-left quadrant of the 1h parameter space. Expected: 120-160 trades, PF 1.2-1.6.
entry_long: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from <= 0 to > 0 (supertrend direction change to bullish).
entry_short: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from >= 0 to < 0 (supertrend direction change to bearish).

exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (9.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Testing 1h ADX sensitivity: does 1h need stricter ADX than 4h due to noise?
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 9.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: SUPERTd_7_3.0 direction flip on 1h — does signal quality hold on shorter timeframe?
ADX_14 > 15 — testing if 1h noise requires stricter ADX than 4h's optimal ADX>10
